dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic edged out the day because Claude/Opus was the only tracked stack that HN kept returning to as something developers were actively paying for, comparing against, and in one case choosing over Copilot, even though commenters also warned that Claude can distance people from understanding their own code and criticized Claude Code's curl|bash install flow [E1][E2][E3][E4].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic won by having the broadest and most consequential direct discussion: one agent thread treated Opus as worth real money and described it as highly productive, another comment said companies were using Claude instead of Copilot, and those positives were stronger than the day's criticisms about developer overreliance and installer safety [E1][E4][E2][E3]. Google Gemini got only a single favorable anecdote, while Microsoft Copilot's clearest signal was tied to frustration that GitHub was prioritizing AI/Copilot over reliability and still not seeing the hoped-for adoption [E5][E6].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude and Opus were discussed repeatedly as the models people were actually using for agentic coding, with one thread praising Opus as productive enough to justify heavy spend and another saying companies were choosing Claude over Copilot; the drag came from worries that Claude use weakens developers' grasp of their own code and from criticism of Claude Code's curl|bash installer [E1][E4][E2][E3].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: A single AI discussion included a firsthand report that Gemini 3 produced really strong results on a retro emulator and assembler with minimal prompting, but the signal was just one anecdote inside a broader 'AI works on easy things, not hard novel ones' thread [E5].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: In the GitHub outage thread, Copilot was framed as part of the problem rather than the upside: commenters said GitHub leadership was over-focused on AI/Copilot while core reliability slipped, and one claimed enterprise adoption was falling short of expectations [E6].

evidence.summary: In the 'Eight more months of agents' discussion, commenters described Opus as very productive and worth substantial spend, while treating Claude/Claude Code as a serious default in agentic coding workflows even if the UX and cheaper models drew complaints.
A top comment on 'Nobody knows how the whole system works' said using Claude makes the author's situational awareness slip and encourages not looking at the code at all.
In the malware-from-Google-search thread, a commenter singled out the new 'native' Claude Code install as another example of unsafe curl|bash installation guidance.
During discussion of another GitHub outage, one commenter said Copilot was not being adopted by companies as hoped and that those companies were using Claude instead.
In the 'AI makes the easy part easier and the hard part harder' thread, a commenter reported 'really great results' from Gemini 3 on a retro emulator and assembler with minimal prompting.
The GitHub outage discussion blamed management's focus on AI and Copilot for neglecting core platform reliability, and one commenter added that Copilot adoption had disappointed companies.
